summarization.a: The etch rate of WO₃ increases with increasing BCl₃ exposure at a constant temperature and HF exposure, and saturates at 4 angstrom per cycle.
summarization.b: The etch rate of WO₃ increases with increasing HF exposure at a constant temperature and BCl₃ exposure, and soft-saturates at 4.2 angstrom per cycle.

data_extraction.a:
| BCl₃ Exposure (mTorr s) | WO₃ Etch Rate (Å/cycle) |
|---|---|
| 0 | 0 |
| 200 | 3.7 |
| 300 | 4.2 |
| 500 | 4.1 |

a.question: If an exposure of 600 mTorr s would have been chosen, what would likely be the etch rate and why?
a.answer: a	The etch rate would also 4 angstrom per cycle. This is the saturated value so further increasing the exposure would not result in a higher etch rate.

a.question: What type of function can be used to fit the given datapoints?
a.answer: a	Usually an exponential function is used to fit the datapoints, where fit parameter in the exponent are related to dosing constants.

b.question: Is full saturation observed within the given range of exposures?
b.answer: b	No, there is no full saturation observed. The curve has not flattened fully.

b.question: At what temperature have the experiments be performed?
